records.ety: [L. occultatio a hiding, fr. occultare, v. intens. of occulere: cf. F. occultation. See Occult.]

defs: The hiding of a heavenly body from sight by the intervention of some other of the heavenly bodies; -- applied especially to eclipses of stars and planets by the moon, and to the eclipses of satellites of planets by their primaries.

quotes: The reappearance of such an author after those long periods of occultation.
